title: Anomaly detection deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Anomaly detection; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Anomaly detection: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? An outlier score is a probability of fraud or danger, and a stricter threshold always improves the detector.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Define unusual relative to a reference and evaluate thresholded alerts with prevalence, review capacity, false alarms, misses, drift, and domain confirmation.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Define unusual relative to a reference and evaluate thresholded alerts with prevalence, review capacity, false alarms, misses, drift, and domain confirmation.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

items.title: Anomaly detection: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'domain confirmation'. Define unusual relative to a reference and evaluate thresholded alerts with prevalence, review capacity, false alarms, misses, drift, and domain confirmation.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Define unusual relative to a reference and evaluate thresholded alerts with prevalence, review capacity, false alarms, misses, drift, and domain confirmation.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.

items.title: Anomaly detection: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read reference values followed by a vertical bar and one probe. Compute median absolute deviation score and print typical or anomalous using score greater than 3.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys
import math
import statistics

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    left, right = data.split('|', 1); values = list(map(float, left.split())); probe = float(right)
    median = statistics.median(values); mad = statistics.median(abs(value-median) for value in values)
    score = abs(probe-median) / mad if mad else (0.0 if probe == median else float('inf'))
    label = 'anomalous' if score > 3 else 'typical'
    rendered = 'inf' if math.isinf(score) else f"{score:.2f}"
    return f"{label} {rendered}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The robust score is explicitly relative to the reference sample and threshold; anomalous is not equated with harmful.
